channel.title: Dispatches from the Substrate — Essays · Nirodha Collective
channel.description: Popular-science essays on the Predator Principle. Bilingual, claim-linked, CC BY 4.0.

item.title: The Biosphere Trauma
item.description: Fear touches no one and still shapes the whole body: fear alone lowers the number of offspring and hardens into the worldview we call realism. We carry the nervous system of a hunted animal — and most of the time we mistake its reflexes for our insights.

item.title: We Are the Meteorite
item.description: Five times an executioner came from outside — asteroid, volcano, poisoned sea. The sixth extinction has no external cause but a principle: us. And we are the first projectile in Earth’s history that can decide, in mid-flight, not to become an impact.

item.title: The Mistake That Eats Itself
item.description: Devouring is a late strategy, not the ground of life — and as the order of everything it is malignant. Nuclear arsenals and the sixth extinction are not a test still pending: they are the running finding.

item.title: The Fermi Paradox That Isn't One
item.description: Where is everybody? We search the sky for predators because we are predators — but spreading is not eating. A mature intelligence does not carry the predatory as an option. The silence is no cage and no gravestone. It is a mirror.
